# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use a base image with a minimal installation of services
FROM alpine:latest

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    POSTGRES_USER=myuser \
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD=mypassword \
    POSTGRES_DB=mydb \
    REDIS_PASSWORD=myredispassword

# Install required packages
RUN apk add --no-cache \
    apache2 \
    php7 \
    php7-fpm \
    postgresql-client \
    redis \
    curl

# Copy application files
COPY . /var/www/html

# Configure Apache and PHP
RUN sed -i 's/Listen 80/Listen 0.0.0.0:80/' /etc/apache2/ports.conf && \
    echo "ServerName localhost" >> /etc/apache2/conf.d/default.conf && \
    sed -i 's/listen = 127.0.0.1/listen = \/var\/run\/php-fpm.sock/' /etc/php7/php-fpm.d/www.conf

# Start services
CMD ["sh", "-c", "service apache2 start & service php-fpm start & redis-server & postgresql -D /var/lib/postgresql/data"]
